rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BOICOURT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satsifeid its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation attributable to defendant. The court did not consider paragraphs 11 and 12 of Mr. Baker¿s declaration because Mr. Baker stated in his deposition that he did not work with Mr. Boicourt at any site where defendant¿s employees were present at a time when both Mr. Boicourt and Mr. Baker were employed by OCF. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BOICOURT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc. SUCCESSOR TO AMCHEM¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. This motion was continued from May 18. The parties did not submit supplemental papers, so the court bases its tentative ruling on the papers filed prior to the initial hearing date. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony shows that he identified a co-worker who saw him using Foster¿s fibrous adhesive, but defendant failed to address that identification in its separate statement and failed to demonstrate that the co-worker does not have knowledge helpful to plaintiff¿s claim. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Baker and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Foster¿s fibrous adhesive) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Sage¿s declaration are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and Mr. Baker¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HRUSKA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Order Granting Kim M. Watterson'S Pro Hac Vice Application
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Detroit Diesel Corporation¿s motion for order granting Kim M. Watterson¿s pro hac vice application is continued on the court¿s own motion to June 8, 2011 at 9:15 a.m. in Department 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)
